title: centella fusca
intro: Centella fusca, also known as lesser marshwort or small waterwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae, native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Centella fusca are attributed to its ability to improve circulation, boost cognitive function, and provide antioxidant protection against oxidative stress and cell damage. Centella fusca can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine and modern supplements.
